primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of foreign currency risk component of intercompany dividend receivable for designation as hedged item in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 establishes the general rule restricting hedged items to external party transactions in consolidated statements. Paragraph 6.3.6 provides an exception permitting intragroup monetary items' foreign currency risk as hedged items when not fully eliminated on consolidation. Paragraph 6.3.7 addresses risk component designation.

supporting_authority.reason: Requires qualitative or quantitative disclosure on how entity determined the risk component designated as hedged item, clarifying documentation requirements for component designation.
Establishes that interest, dividends and returns on financial liabilities are expenses, relevant to understanding the nature of dividend-related monetary items.

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses net investment hedge accounting but focuses on parent-foreign operation structure rather than intercompany dividend receivables, making it tangentially related.
Addresses dividend payable measurement but concerns non-cash asset distributions, not foreign currency hedge designation eligibility.
Covers provisions and contingencies, not hedge accounting eligibility for foreign currency risk components.

treatment_families.family: Exception permitting intragroup monetary item foreign currency risk as hedged item
General prohibition on intragroup hedged items in consolidated statements
Risk component designation as hedged item

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item hedge exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 explicitly permits foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged item in consolidated statements when exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation, providing a specific exception to the general rule.

approaches.label: External party hedge requirement
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 establishes the default rule that only items with external parties can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements, directly addressing intercompany transactions.

approaches.label: Risk component designation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.7 permits designation of a component of an item as hedged item, which is relevant when considering whether foreign exchange risk component of dividend receivable qualifies separately.
